surface.name: incident.io Slack installation and first declared incident
surface.selection_basis: The official help center presents Slack and Microsoft Teams as peer getting-started surfaces. The Slack installation and incident-declaration guides together document a complete path from Sign in with Slack through app installation to a dedicated first-incident channel, but the vendor does not select Slack, a declaration interface, or live, test, tutorial, or retrospective mode as a universal default.

alternatives_considered.name: Microsoft Teams getting started
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The help center presents Microsoft Teams as a peer chat surface with a different installation and interaction path.

alternatives_considered.name: Tutorial incident
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The five-minute `/inc tutorial` guide is designed for practice rather than producing the same first real-incident terminal state.

alternatives_considered.name: Test incident
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A test incident avoids announcement and reporting side effects, but it is not posted in the main incidents channel or incident library and therefore changes the observable completion signal.

alternatives_considered.name: Browser declaration with inc.new
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The browser form is a documented peer declaration interface; the candidate uses Slack because it follows directly from the documented Slack installation route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an incident.io account through Slack, install the incident.io app, declare a first live incident, and observe the dedicated incident channel and main-channel announcement.
assumed_prior_state: No incident.io account, organization, connected integration, installed Slack app, main incidents channel, or declared incident is assumed.
The candidate assumes a user in a company Slack workspace with a corporate email address and access to a Slack Workspace Admin for installation.
Slack either permits public-channel creation or an administrator can use one of incident.io's documented channel-permission recovery paths.
No signup, authorization, app installation, channel creation, or incident declaration was performed during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A first live incident has been declared and incident.io has created its dedicated Slack incident channel and posted the default heads-up in the main incidents channel.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The dedicated incident Slack channel opens or appears, and a heads-up appears in the main incidents channel.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The declaration guide's What happens next section says a dedicated Slack channel is created and, by default, a heads-up is posted in the main incidents channel as soon as an incident is declared.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable product outcome after identity, installation, and declaration. Video calls, linked tickets, workflow actions, incident coordination, resolution, and post-incident work are conditional or occur after this point.

prerequisites.requirement: Use Sign in with Slack from a company workspace with a corporate email address; personal email signup is restricted.
A Slack administrator is required to grant the app permissions used by incident.io.
The workspace must permit incident.io to create the main incidents channel and per-incident public channels, either through general channel-creation permission or the documented admin-user option.
Choose Slack or Microsoft Teams, then choose a live, test, tutorial, or retrospective incident and a slash-command, message, or browser declaration interface.

primary_path.action: Open the official incident.io help center and locate the getting-started and incident-declaration paths.
primary_path.output: Slack and Microsoft Teams getting-started surfaces and incident-response guidance are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The official Slack installation and declaring-incidents guides can be opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The help center does not identify one chat surface as universal.

primary_path.action: Choose Slack or Microsoft Teams as the connected chat surface.
details: The candidate below resolves this choice with Slack because its installation guide publicly enumerates signup, integration selection, authorization, channel creation, and installation completion.
primary_path.success_signal: The chosen chat platform's getting-started guide is open.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The surfaces have different identity, permission, installation, and interaction steps.

primary_path.action: Choose the incident mode and declaration interface for the first success.
details: Official choices include live, test, tutorial, and retrospective incidents, with Slack command, message shortcut, browser, and other mode-specific entry points.
primary_path.output: One incident route and its observable terminal state are selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The candidate selects a live incident declared by Slack slash command.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Test and tutorial modes intentionally produce different visibility and reporting behavior from a live incident.

candidate_paths.name: Slack signup, app installation, and first live incident
candidate_paths.selection_basis: The official Slack installation page documents the three setup stages, including account creation and terminal installation state, and the official declaring guide documents a slash-command declaration through its dedicated-channel result.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: This resolves primary step 2 with Slack and primary step 3 with a live incident started by `/inc` from a Slack channel.

steps.action: Open Installing incident.io in 30 seconds and follow its linked Sign Up entry.
steps.output: The incident.io Slack signup screen is available.
steps.success_signal: Sign in with Slack is displayed.
steps.failure_or_wait: The Microsoft Teams getting-started route is separate and is not part of this candidate.

steps.action: Select Sign in with Slack and choose the intended Slack Workspace, not the Enterprise organization container.
details: If no workspace appears, sign into the desired Slack workspace in the browser first.
The user must use a corporate or company email address rather than a personal email.
steps.output: Slack displays the restricted permissions requested for account creation.
steps.success_signal: The intended workspace name is visible on the authorization screen.
steps.failure_or_wait: If Slack reports `no_bot_scopes_requested`, use the workspace selector to switch from the Enterprise organization to a Workspace.

steps.action: Review and authorize the Slack identity permissions requested by incident.io.
details: The installation guide says incident.io creates an account and stores basic information such as the user's name and avatar.
steps.output: An incident.io account is created for the Slack user.
steps.success_signal: The setup flow advances to integration selection.
steps.failure_or_wait: If signup is rejected because of the email type, retry with a company email or use the documented support contact.

steps.action: Select the integrations the organization uses and continue.
details: This setup step tells incident.io which integrations are commonly used; the guide says full integration setup can be completed later.
steps.output: The Add incident.io to Slack step is displayed.
steps.success_signal: The Slack installation control is available.
steps.failure_or_wait: No integration connection is required at this step of the documented install path.

steps.output: Slack opens the app-install authorization flow.
steps.success_signal: The intended workspace and requested app permissions are shown.
steps.failure_or_wait: A Slack administrator is required because of Slack's permission model.

steps.action: As a Slack administrator, authorize the incident.io app for the intended workspace.
details: If public-channel creation is restricted, either allow everyone except guests to create public channels and select I've changed channel creation permissions, or connect a dedicated Slack admin user through I'd like to use a Slack admin user and Continue.
steps.input: Slack administrator authorization and a permitted channel-creation policy
steps.output: Slack grants the app permissions and returns to incident.io.
steps.success_signal: The install completes and the workspace contains the incident.io app and main incidents channel.
steps.failure_or_wait: Channel restrictions must be resolved with one of the two documented permission paths before incident.io can create its required channels.

steps.action: Verify that incident.io is installed and that the main incidents channel exists in Slack.
details: The installation guide says this channel is created automatically when setup completes.
steps.output: The workspace is ready for incident.io commands and first-incident announcements.
steps.success_signal: The incident.io app is installed and the main incidents channel is visible.
steps.failure_or_wait: If `/inc` or `/incident` invokes another app, Slack uses the most recently installed app for duplicate commands; use the incident.io app entry point or resolve the collision.

steps.action: In any non-incident Slack channel, type `/inc Website is down` and press Enter.
details: The title following `/inc` prefills the incident form; `/incident` is the equivalent long command.
steps.output: The declare-incident form opens with the title prefilled.
steps.success_signal: The incident details form is visible.
steps.failure_or_wait: A workspace can restrict which users may declare particular incident types even though declaration is available to everyone by default.

steps.action: Keep the incident in live mode and complete every field the form marks as required.
details: Incident forms are fully customizable, so exact fields differ by organization; the guide says an incident can be declared with only a few details.
Private incidents are a separate option when the organization has enabled them.
steps.input: Prefilled title and all workspace-required incident details
steps.output: The form is ready to declare a live incident.
steps.success_signal: All required fields are satisfied and Declare is available.
steps.failure_or_wait: If a required incident type is unavailable, an administrator may have restricted declaration access for that type.

steps.output: incident.io declares the incident and creates its default Slack response surfaces.
steps.success_signal: A dedicated incident channel is created and a heads-up appears in the main incidents channel.
steps.failure_or_wait: Video calls, linked tickets, and workflow actions occur only when the corresponding providers and workflows are configured; they are not required for this candidate's first success.

steps.action: Open the dedicated incident channel and confirm the main-channel heads-up.
steps.output: The first incident's collaboration space and default announcement are observable.
steps.success_signal: The dedicated channel is available for responders and the main incidents channel contains the incident announcement.
steps.failure_or_wait: Private-incident visibility and customized workflows can change who sees the result and what additional actions occur.

branches.path: Follow the separate Microsoft Teams getting-started and installation route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes identity, authorization, installation, commands, and the observable communication surface.

branches.condition: The user wants a guided practice route
branches.path: In Slack, type `/inc tutorial` and follow the approximately five-minute tutorial incident.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Provides a guided, non-standard practice outcome rather than the candidate's first live incident.

branches.condition: The user wants to avoid live announcement and reporting effects
branches.path: Use `/incident test` in Slack or select the Test tab in the dashboard.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The test incident is not announced in the main incidents channel and does not appear in the incident library or Insights.

branches.path: Use `/inc retro`, the dashboard, or the API to create a retrospective incident.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Records a past incident rather than starting a live response.

branches.condition: The user starts from an existing Slack message
branches.path: Open the message's three-dot menu, select Connect to apps, then Create an incident; use More message shortcuts if the action is not visible.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Prefills or anchors the declaration from existing Slack context rather than a slash command.

branches.condition: The user declares from a browser
branches.path: Open inc.new, choose live, retrospective, or test, complete the form, and select Declare.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Moves form interaction to the browser while retaining the configured Slack outputs.

friction_gates.description: Slack and Microsoft Teams are peer getting-started surfaces with different installation and interaction paths.
Slack signup is restricted to corporate or company email addresses.
A Slack administrator must authorize the app because of Slack's permission model.
Restricted public-channel creation requires a workspace policy change or a connected Slack admin user before incident.io can create required channels.
Selecting an Enterprise organization rather than a Workspace can produce the documented `no_bot_scopes_requested` authorization error.
Live, test, tutorial, and retrospective modes intentionally change visibility, reporting, and completion signals.
Incident forms and required fields are customizable by organization, and admins can restrict access to incident types.
Duplicate `/inc` or `/incident` slash commands can invoke the most recently installed Slack app instead of incident.io.

time_to_first_success.value: The Slack installation page is titled Installing incident.io in 30 seconds and the declaration guide says declaring takes seconds; neither claim covers the entire no-account route through the first incident.

sources.title: Help center - incident.io
evidence_supported: Slack and Microsoft Teams peer surfaces

sources.title: Installing incident.io in 30 seconds - incident.io
evidence_supported: Sign in with Slack account creation

sources.title: Declaring incidents - incident.io
evidence_supported: conditional video, ticket, and workflow outputs

uncertainties.question: Should the representative incident.io first mile use Slack or Microsoft Teams?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes identity, admin authorization, installation, interaction, and terminal communication surfaces.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official help center presents both as peer getting-started routes and does not designate one universal default.

uncertainties.question: Should first success be a live, test, tutorial, or retrospective incident?
uncertainties.impact: The modes intentionally differ in announcements, incident-library visibility, Insights inclusion, and response side effects.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The incident-mode documentation explains each mode but leaves the safety and visibility choice to the organization or user.

uncertainties.question: Which declaration interface should represent the general path?
uncertainties.impact: Slash command, Slack message, and browser entry require different interactions and inputs before the same or similar incident outcome.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The declaration guide documents these as alternatives without selecting one for all users.

uncertainties.question: Which fields and incident types will a fresh organization's form expose?
uncertainties.impact: The number of actions and permission checks before Declare can vary.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Incident forms are fully customizable and administrators can restrict incident-type declaration permissions.
